extra.reason: All deliverables implemented and verified: root docker-compose.yml (include: pattern), .env, Makefile, compose/kafka.yml, compose/minio.yml, compose/nessie.yml, kafka/init-topics.sh, minio/init-bucket.sh. docker compose config validates. docker compose up: kafka, schema-registry, minio, nessie reach healthy; kafka-init and minio-init exit 0 idempotently. Verified topics shopify.products/shopify.inventory exist, warehouse bucket exists, Nessie API responds on :19120. Fixed a RocksDB permission-denied issue on fresh named volumes by adding a nessie-init chown step (image runs as non-root uid 10000). Committed as ccb0c7a.

Implemented flink/Dockerfile (Flink 1.20.1 + Kafka avro-confluent connector, iceberg-flink-runtime, iceberg-aws-bundle, iceberg-nessie w/ resolved transitives, hadoop-client-api/-runtime), flink/sql/ingest.sql (Kafka sources -> Nessie/Iceberg sinks, format-version=2, 60s checkpointing), and compose/flink.yml (jobmanager/taskmanager/flink-sql-submit, UI on 8082). Verified: docker build succeeds, docker compose config validates, SQL parses/executes through catalog creation on the built image. Live e2e ingestion deferred to shopify-lakehouse-iiv.5 since sibling infra/generator services aren't merged yet.

Implemented schemas/product.avsc + inventory_level.avsc, generator/ Go module (config/model/gen/producer packages + cmd/generator), config.yaml, generator/Dockerfile, compose/generator.yml. go build/go vet/go test ./... all pass on host (confluent-kafka-go/v2 builds librdkafka from bundled C source via cgo, no system package needed). docker build succeeds; smoke-tested container binary attempts real Kafka+SR connections and fails/retries correctly when unreachable (expected, live e2e deferred). Committed as 98d54e4. Mailed epic thread flagging compose/generator.yml's external:true network assumption (root compose.yml doesn't exist yet in this worktree) and the go 1.25 toolchain requirement pulled in by confluent-kafka-go/v2's deps.

Implemented Dremio query layer: compose/dremio.yml (dremio-oss service on port 9047 with named volume + sane JVM heap, plus a one-shot dremio-bootstrap service using curlimages/curl), dremio/bootstrap.sh (idempotent Dremio REST API script: creates the admin user if not yet bootstrapped, logs in, and registers the Nessie catalog as a Dremio NESSIE-type source with MinIO S3-compatible storage settings — skips work that's already done so make up reaches queryable tables without UI clicks), and dremio/setup.md (exact manual click-path/values + troubleshooting fallback in case the REST schema drifts across Dremio versions). Root docker-compose.yml and .env updated additively only (one include line; three new DREMIO_ADMIN_* keys, no existing keys touched), coordinated via Agent Mail with impl-shopify-lakehouse-iiv.3 who also held docker-compose.yml. Verified standalone: docker compose config validates cleanly (all services including dremio/dremio-bootstrap render correctly); shellcheck clean on bootstrap.sh. Live Nessie-source/query verification deferred to the e2e issue (iiv.5) per shared contract. Committed on feat/shopify-lakehouse-iiv.4-dremio (not merged to epic branch — orchestrator will handle that).

Implemented Spark compaction scheduled loop: spark/Dockerfile (apache/spark 3.5.4 + iceberg-spark-runtime-3.5_2.12 1.9.1 + nessie-spark-extensions-3.5_2.12 0.103.3 + hadoop-aws/aws-java-sdk-bundle 3.3.4/1.12.262 for S3A), spark/compact.py (rewrite_data_files bin-pack, rewrite_manifests, expire_snapshots with bounded retention against nessie.lakehouse.products and nessie.lakehouse.inventory_levels), spark/entrypoint.sh (loop every $COMPACTION_INTERVAL, default 10m; one-shot mode via COMPACTION_ONE_SHOT=true), compose/spark.yml service, and 'make compact-now' target. Wired into root docker-compose.yml include list; added additive-only COMPACTION_* keys to .env. Verified standalone: docker build succeeds, docker compose config validates, and a live standalone run of the built image executed spark-submit -> compact.py -> loaded Iceberg/Nessie Spark extensions -> built SparkCatalog -> attempted rewrite_data_files with a real network call to Nessie (failed only due to no live Nessie/MinIO in the standalone run, as expected). No Python/classpath errors. Full e2e run against the live stack deferred to shopify-lakehouse-iiv.5. Committed on branch feat/shopify-lakehouse-iiv.3-spark-compaction (5dbef25); not merged into epic branch per orchestrator ownership.

Verified: compact.py calls rewrite_data_files(binpack), rewrite_manifests, and expire_snapshots for both lakehouse.products and lakehouse.inventory_levels (PASS). Dockerfile includes iceberg-spark-runtime-3.5_2.12-1.9.1.jar, nessie-spark-extensions-3.5_2.12-0.103.3.jar, and hadoop-aws-3.3.4.jar (PASS). compose/spark.yml wired to lakehouse network with NESSIE_URI, S3_ENDPOINT, AWS credentials, COMPACTION_INTERVAL env vars (PASS). entrypoint.sh loops with 'while true; do spark-submit compact.py; sleep COMPACTION_INTERVAL; done' (PASS). docker compose config parses cleanly (PASS). Stack partially running: spark-compaction and dremio are up, but nessie/minio/kafka are exited — spark-compaction logs show connection errors to nessie and 'Compaction run finished in 7.5s' (error path, not success). Nessie API at localhost:19120 unreachable (Nessie container exited). Live compaction cannot be verified without restarting nessie+minio. Static code review: fully PASS. Runtime verification: BLOCKED by nessie/minio being down.
